channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Plane burst into flames after skidding off runway at an airport in South Korea, killing at least 85
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skid off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy, killing at least 85 people, officials said, in one of the country's worst aviation disa
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Charles Dolan, HBO and Cablevision founder, dies at 98
item.description: Charles Dolan, who founded some of the most prominent U.S. media companies including Home Box Office Inc. and Cablevision Systems Corp., has died at age 98.
item.dc:creator: Brian P. D. Hannon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Texas mayor confirms fatality from Brazoria County tornado
item.description: "Please join me in praying for all those impacted," Mayor Davis shared in a statement on social media, expressing condolences and support for the affected community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Estrada, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colorado man accused of attacking TV reporter, asking him if he's a US citizen
item.description: A Colorado man allegedly attacked a television news reporter after asking if he was a citizen, saying, "This is Trump's America now," court documents show, as reported by the Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Kids learning magic of science with dancing candy canes
item.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Local kids are learning the magic of science while having some festive fun on Saturday. Roughly 30 kids filled the Lackawanna County Children's Library on Saturday. They gathered for their Curiosity Club's Candy Cane Science Event.
item.dc:creator: Julia Priest, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KREX 5

item.title: Videos show apparent tornadoes moving through Mississippi
item.description: FRANKLIN COUNTY, Miss. (WJTV) - An apparent tornado moved through Franklin County on Saturday, December 28. Karla Wallace, a WJTV 12 News viewer, submitted a video that showed the storm moving through the McCall Creek area.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, NewsNation

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida train slams into fire truck, leaving firefighters, passengers injured
item.description: A train in Florida collided on Saturday with a fire rescue truck, leaving three firefighters and 12 passengers in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will New Year's Eve be loud or quiet? What are the top 2025 resolutions? AP-NORC poll has answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you're planning on ringing in the new year quietly at home, you're not alone. A majority of U.S adults intend to celebrate New Year’s Eve at home, according to a new poll by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani’s contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump as two Georgia election poll workers try to collect a $148 million defamation award the
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside a New York City subway train, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a sleeping woman to death inside a New York City subway train has been indicted on murder and arson charges, prosecutors announced Friday, as authorities continue working to identify the victim.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These movies are some of 2025's most anticipated
item.description: With the vast majority of 2024 in the rearview mirror, it's time to start looking at the most anticipated films primed to take the silver screen in 2025. Full of adaptations, sequels, and reboots of beloved properties, viewers will not have difficulty finding something familiar to enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: The State Department says an American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lava erupts from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: OpenAI whistleblower's mother wants suicide death investigation reopened
item.description: OpenAI whistleblower Suchir Balaji's death was ruled a suicide — but his family is calling on police to reopen their investigation, saying it "doesn't look like a normal situation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Couple finds winning $1M Powerball ticket on Christmas Day, hours before expiration
item.description: A Kendallville couple is $1 million dollars richer after finding the winning Powerball ticket just hours before it was set to expire.
item.dc:creator: Marin Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SEE IT: ‘Peanut-shaped’ roundabout ready to open in Ohio
item.description: In a post to the city's Facebook page, officials offered "a few pointers to help drivers navigate safely.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal migrant indicted on murder charges after woman is burned to death on subway
item.description: The illegal migrant accused of burning a New York City subway rider to death on Sunday has been charged with murder and other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe survives close brush with the sun's scorching surface
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NASA's Parker Solar Probe has successfully made the closest approach to the sun, the space agency confirmed Friday. Earlier this week, the spacecraft passed within a record-breaking 3.8 million miles (6 million kilometers) of the scorching star.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk stirs pot on X over 'shortage' of talented American engineers
item.description: Musk, an immigrant from South Africa, posted to X that there is a shortage of "talented" engineers in the U.S. and companies should start looking to other countries.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More Americans say they need a break from political news: Survey
item.description: Most adults want a break from political news due to overload and fatigue, according to a new survey from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Average mortgage rates close 2024 at the highest since July
item.description: The housing market will end the year with its highest 30-year mortgage rate since July at 6.85%, according to data from Freddie Mac.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Where is Santa? How to track his Christmas 2024 journey
item.description: Santa Claus, St. Nicholas, Babbo Natale, Père Noël — whatever you call him, the man clad in red will be here soon.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
